book.title_en: A Glass Bridge Between Two Worlds
book.title_ja: ビーズの魔法：ムラノと京都をつなぐガラスの橋
book.author: Maurizio Lotter

editorial_principle: A Glass Bridge is not a book about glass. It is a field in which two craft traditions — Venetian and Japanese — coexist without merging, held together by a single practitioner's body and a single object: the bead. The book is bilingual because the field it describes is irreducibly dual.

fields.label: campo_artigianale_bilingue
fields.label_ja: 二言語的工芸フィールド
fields.basho: the bilingual craft field does not translate one tradition into the other: it holds both simultaneously as active perceptual layers — Venetian technique and Japanese spatial sensibility coexist in each bead
fields.kokyu: the rhythm is that of thirty-plus years of practice in the intersection: neither tradition dominates, both are continuously active
fields.activation_condition: encounter with any object that carries two traditions simultaneously without resolving into one
fields.persistence_rule: the bilingual field remains active as a permanent perceptual disposition
fields.dissolution_rule: non_dissolvibile: structural condition of the entire system
fields.textual_anchor_ja: 伝統、革新、そして情熱が交差する

fields.label: campo_corpo_tecnico
fields.basho: the technical body of the master is the irreducible field of craft transmission: what Lotter knows cannot be fully said — it lives in the hands, in the timing, in the relationship with heat
fields.kokyu: the rhythm of embodied knowledge is that of years of repetition: the technique becomes invisible, the body becomes the technique
fields.activation_condition: any description of craft technique that exceeds linguistic capture
fields.persistence_rule: embodied knowledge remains permanently active in the practitioner
fields.dissolution_rule: non_dissolvibile: embodied skill cannot be unlearned

affects.field-01: embodied knowledge sustains the bilingual field as its enabling condition

fields.textual_anchor_en: techniques refined over centuries, along with the deep emotions and dedication of master artisans
fields.textual_anchor_ja: 何世紀にもわたって磨かれてきた技法と、名匠たちの深い情熱と献身

fields.label: campo_oggetto_irrisolvibile
fields.label_ja: 非解決的オブジェクトフィールド
fields.basho: the Lotter bead is an aesthetically irresolvable object: it cannot be classified as Venetian or Japanese — it occupies both without belonging to either
fields.kokyu: the rhythm is oscillation between incompatible classificatory systems that both apply and both fail
fields.activation_condition: perception of a bead containing dual cultural origin without synthesis
fields.persistence_rule: irresolvability is a permanent structural condition of the object
fields.textual_anchor_en: Lotter's bead is a Venetian bead crafted using the same technique as the Franchini masters, but it tells an Eastern story.
fields.textual_anchor_ja: ロッテルのビーズは、フランキーニ兄弟の技法で作られたヴェネツィアのビーズですが、東洋の世界を語ります。

fields.label: campo_patrimonio_vivente
fields.label_ja: 生きた遺産フィールド
fields.basho: living heritage is not a museum condition: it is a field that exists only through continuous embodied transmission — Murano survives only in practice
fields.kokyu: the rhythm of heritage is fragile continuity: Franchini → Murano tradition → Lotter → Kyoto — a chain that can break at any point
fields.activation_condition: recognition that craft exists only while it is actively practiced
fields.persistence_rule: the field remains active only through continuous transmission
fields.dissolution_rule: dissolvibile: interruption of transmission collapses the field
fields.textual_anchor_en: heritage, culture, and the relentless pursuit of beauty
fields.textual_anchor_ja: 遺産、文化、そして美を追求する終わりなき探求

system_note.system_behavior: four coupled perceptual fields forming a single irreversible craft ontology: language, body, object, heritage
